title: Assistive Technology Economics for Neurodivergent Populations
abstract: Cost-benefit analysis of open-source assistive tech

body.text: This paper examines the economic impact of open-source assistive technology for neurodivergent individuals, demonstrating how affordability and accessibility can improve outcomes and reduce societal costs.

Existing assistive technology is often prohibitively expensive, creating barriers for neurodivergent individuals who are disproportionately unemployed and uninsured. Open-source models can dramatically reduce costs while increasing accessibility.
Open-source reduces costs by 80-90% compared to proprietary alternatives
Increased accessibility leads to better employment and educational outcomes
Community-driven innovation improves relevance and usability
P31 Labs' commitment to open-source AGPLv3/MIT licensing and sub-$50 hardware targets creates sustainable, scalable assistive technology for neurodivergent communities.
